rulings.Case Title: MARION L STARR VS. AGNES C KOSEL ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday April 13, 2018, line 1. DEFENDANT AGNES KOZEL MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT moot. = (501/ACM)

rulings.Case Title: MARION L STARR VS. AGNES C KOSEL ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday April 13, 2018, line 1. DEFENDANT AGNES KOZEL DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT SUSTAINED with leave to amend causes of action for interference with prescriptive easement, declaratory relief and fraud. Defendant's request for judicial notice is granted. Plaintiff shall have 10 days from the notice of entry of order to file an amended complaint (1) reconciling allegations in the complaint and judicially noticeable documents; (2) alleging fraud with requisite particularity without relying on matters subject to Evid. Code 1152(a) and/or alleging facts showing that such matters are not subject to Evid. Code 1152(a) (e.g. SAC paragraph 18). = (501/ACM)

rulings.Case Title: OMAR SHAFIE VS. MEGHAN MCGARRY DANGANAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Discovery And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendants Jobe Danganan And Meghan Mcgarry
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday April 13, 2018, line 2. PLAINTIFF OMAR SHAFIE Motion To Compel Discovery And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendants Jobe Danganan And Meghan Mcgarry DENIED. Discovery requests, as drafted, are too broad. Plaintiff may serve properly framed discovery requests. = (501/ACM)

rulings.Case Title: BORREGAS COURT ASSOCIATES, A CALIFORNIA GENERAL VS. BOSTON FINANCIAL INVESTMENT MANAGEMENT A ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday April 13, 2018, line 3. DEFENDANT BFIM-CALIFORNIA ACQUISITION CORP., ERRONEOUSLY, EDISON CAPITAL HOUSING INVESTMENTS, EDISON CAPITAL HOUSING PARTNERS, XI, LLC DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT OFF CALENDAR per stipulation and order. = (501/ACM)

rulings.Case Title: YAMEN ELTAWIL VS. KIRANSINH THAKOR ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday April 13, 2018, line 4. DEFENDANT KIRANSINH THAKOR, BALBOA HOTEL, LLC, 112 HYDE ST LLC DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT SUSTAINED with leave to amend. Plaintiff shall have 10 days to amend the complaint to address the following: (1) alter ego allegations in paragraph 11; if Plaintiff is not alleging this paragraph as to defendant 112 Hyde (as suggested in opposition) Plaintiff shall so clarify in the complaint; (2) to clarify that the first and second causes of action do not address 2012 transfer or to allege each transfer in a separate causes of action and specify which defendants are named for fraudulent transfer allegedly occurring (a) in 2012 (facts regarding tolling of the statute of limitations/delayed discovery to be alleged) and (b) in 2017; (3) to allege facts in support of allegations of actual intent; lack of receipt of reasonably equivalent value for the property; and fraud with particularity. = (501/ACM)

rulings.Case Title: FRANCISCA B POBRE VS. TAMMY M BUHLER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Order 1) Deeming Admitted The Items In Defendant'S Request For Admission Of Facts And Genuiness Of Documents, Set One (Or In The Alternative Compeeling Plaintiff'S Response) 2) Compelling Plaintiff'S Response To Form Interrogatories 3) Awarding Monetary
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday April 13, 2018, line 6. DEFENDANT TAMMY BUHLER Notice Of Motion And Motion To Order 1) Deeming Admitted The Items In Defendant'S Request For Admission Of Facts And Genuiness Of Documents, Set One (Or In The Alternative Compeeling Plaintiff'S Response) 2) Compelling Plaintiff'S Response To Form Interrogatories 3) Awarding Monetary GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Motion is GRANTED, subject to opposition as to the Form Interrogatories. Plaintiff to provide complete verified responses without objections within 5 days of notice of entry of order. Motion is DENIED as to Requests for Admissions. No evidence provided that requests for admissions have been served in this case filed on March 13, 2018. Counsel declares under penalty of perjury that she served requests for admission on November 6, 2014. = (501/ACM)
